metrics.narrative: Sherald Yap framed the problem qualitatively on the 19 Nov 2025 intro call: speed on low-risk/'fuel spend' construction items, eliminating 2-3 page manual BR inputs, and centralising procurement so non-procurement business users stop doing buying work. She noted the incumbent stack was itself a seven-figure investment ('iRequest ... also 1,000,000 dollar') and that any change must justify spending 'another million dollars to buy a new one.' No hard ROI, savings %, or cycle-time target has been quantified in the read evidence, so the value case remains qualitative. This qualitative gap is the most likely root cause of the EXCO stall — quantifying the savings/efficiency gain with Sherald's data is the move.

sources: Next_Step__c (strong leaning to Zycus; non competitive)
champion.narrative: Sherald Yap is the working champion: she drove the deal from the 19 Nov 2025 intro through the Jan 2026 business-case/ROI workshops, signalled 'strong leaning to Zycus' and 'non competitive'. But her access to the EXCO/CFO who controls budget is unproven, and the ~5-month approval stall plus ~109 days since the last incoming buyer email suggest she is blocked or lacks the authority to unblock EXCO. Strong advocate, unproven power reach — diagnose the blocker, re-arm her with a CFO-ready number, and have her broker the budget-owner conversation.

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget decision sits with EXCO and is the live blocker: Next_Step__c shows the business case went to management for sign-off (23 Jan 2026) and as of 1 Jun 2026 the deal is 'waiting on budget validation internally.' On the 19 Nov call Sherald referenced needing top-management alignment and that a CFO would demand 'a business case ... an ROI.' But no individual economic buyer (CFO or EXCO sponsor) is mapped as an engaged contact role, and none appears as a named speaker on any read call. Sherald is a mid-level Procurement Manager and cannot clear an EXCO budget decision alone — reaching the budget owner, brokered through Sherald, is the single highest-leverage gap to close.

decision_process.narrative: (unchanged) 19 Nov 2025 overview -> 8 Dec 2025 F2F with procurement + IT -> 14-23 Jan 2026 value-justification and final ROI/business-case workshop -> management sign-off targeted 'by 30th Jan' -> as of 1 Jun 2026 'KIV waiting on budget validation internally.'
